title: Life expectancy gap for Black Bostonians is growing, health officials warn
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: The life expectancy gap between Black Bostonians and others doubled from 3.3 to 6.6 years between 2013 and 2024.
Black Bostonians' leading causes of premature death are unintentional overdose, cancer, and cardiometabolic disease, according to a city report.
Boston Public Health Commission announced a $2 million investment in health initiatives for Black men and partnership with Dana-Farber.

title: Former Celtics center Luke Kornet calls out Hawks over ‘Magic City’ promotion
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Luke Kornet criticized the Atlanta Hawks' 'Magic City Night' promotion for its associations with a popular strip club.
Kornet urged the NBA to reconsider the event, emphasizing the importance of respecting and protecting women in the league.
Magic City Night will feature performances by rapper T.I. and serve lemon pepper wings at the March 16 Hawks game.

title: Married couple from R.I. identified as victims in fatal Swansea crash
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Carolyn Carcasi, 54, and James Carcasi, 53, from Bristol, Rhode Island, were killed in a Swansea crash on February 27.
Demitri Sousa, 28, allegedly hit the couple's car on Route 136 after allegedly shooting a man in Rhode Island.
Sousa's car severely damaged the Carcasis' Subaru, which burst into flames, killing both occupants on the scene.

title: Steve Kerr apologizes for comments about Kristaps Porzingis’ lingering illness
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Steve Kerr apologized for discussing Kristaps Porzingis' medical condition, admitting it was beyond his expertise.
Porzingis has played only 18 games this season due to illness and was recently traded to the Warriors.
Porzingis was diagnosed with POTS but Kerr claimed misinformation on it; Porzingis now battles an unspecified illness.

title: Taking stock of Bruins’ top trade chips entering the NHL deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins have four first-round picks over the next two years, including a valuable one from Toronto.
Defenseman Mason Lohrei and forward Casey Mittelstadt are potential trade chips for the Bruins before the deadline.
Veteran players like Andrew Peeke and Viktor Arvidsson may be moved as the Bruins explore trade options.

title: NFL insider: Maxx Crosby trade could happen ‘this week’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Maxx Crosby could be traded this week, with several teams including the Patriots interested, according to Albert Breer.
The Raiders reportedly prefer not to actively shop Crosby, but expect to receive incoming trade calls, says Tom Pelissero.
Crosby, 29, is a key player for the Raiders, who hold the No. 1 draft pick after a 3-14 season.

title: Quincy man sentenced in fatal St. Patrick’s Day assault in downtown Boston
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Sanusi Sadiq, 31, sentenced to 3-6 years for manslaughter in 2023 St. Patrick's Day assault in Boston.
Victim Barry Whelan, found outside a Winter Street ATM, died from blunt force trauma after confrontation.
Retrial conviction followed a mistrial; racial slur sparked argument leading to Whelan's fatal injury.

title: Bill Simmons seems convinced that ‘all signs are pointing to’ this date for Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Bill Simmons speculates Jayson Tatum will return Friday against the Mavericks at TD Garden.
Jayson Tatum has been cleared for 5-on-5 practice but remains out for Tuesday's game against the Bucks.
The Celtics will face Maine native Cooper Flagg and the Mavericks in a nationally televised game Friday.

title: Should teens be allowed to carry handguns in Massachusetts?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court to decide on legality of handgun possession for those under 21.
The case challenges Massachusetts law following the Supreme Court's 2022 ruling enhancing public firearm carry rights.
Massachusetts law currently allows handgun licenses only for individuals aged 21 and older.

title: 5 takeaways from Don Sweeney’s Bruins press conference before trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney is open to trades but aims to balance short-term gains with long-term team development.
Sweeney prefers not to engage in rental player trades, focusing instead on long-term acquisitions for the Bruins.
Boston may pursue impactful players under long-term contracts, despite high costs, to bolster their playoff prospects.
